title: ProLogium Technology Teams Up with Germany's FEV Group at CES to Showcase Next-Generation Solid-State Battery Module
content: ProLogium Technology and FEV Group are co-exhibiting at CES 2026 to showcase a next-generation EV battery module built on ProLogium's proprietary superfluidized all-inorganic solid-state lithium ceramic battery technology. This collaboration combines ProLogium's cell innovation with FEV's expertise in battery system design and thermal management. The module offers flexible configurations: a "maximum range" option targeting approximately 1,000 km, or a "maximum lightweight" option that reduces vehicle weight by up to 300 kg. The technology also supports ultra-fast charging, potentially achieving 60-80% capacity in just 4-6 minutes. ProLogium CEO Vincent Yang stated that this partnership advances 1,000-km range from concept to product-ready reality, supported by their planned gigafactory in Dunkirk, France. FEV CEO Dr. Patrick Hupperich noted that their joint efforts ensure these technologies are scalable and safe for mass production. The companies have been collaborating since 2022 to drive solid-state commercialization.
author: Analysts Account
